NAME: SYLENE’S CRYSTAL
DESCRIPTION: A delicate, ornate steel wand with a single inlaid gem at the tip. The color of the gemstone determines the bonus: • Rose: +2 FOCUS • Yellow: +2 CREATIVITY • Royal Blue: +2 LOGIC • Vermillion: +2 INSIGHT • Cloudy White: +2 BODY • Multicolor: +2 CHARM

NAME: CHAMPION’S WAND
DESCRIPTION: A classic styled birch wand woven with griffin mane hair. Gives +2 to three different Abilities, chosen at creation.

NAME: WHISPERED SECRETS
DESCRIPTION: An untrimmed yew wand brushed with hellclaw sap and smoked over an enchanted flame, giving it a mysterious enchanted blackness that curls the bark in places, but is still smooth to the touch. It has an aura of ancient secrets. Gives +4 to a given Ability, chosen at creation.

NAME: ODIN’S SIGHT
DESCRIPTION: An untrimmed ash wand you can feel a connection to the earth through. While this wand is active, you can use it to gain one piece of magical insight into a person, place, object, or situation (AP2). This ability, once used, cannot be used again for 6 hours.

NAME: INDOMITABLE
DESCRIPTION: A thick redwood wand reinforced with brass bands. While this wand is active, you have a +1 to all Resist checks.

NAME: MISTY VISION
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from wisteria wood, with enchanted wisteria flowers at the tip that never wither. While this wand is active, you can use it to cast Minor Phantasm with a roll of 12. Once you do, the flowers close and take 3 hours to blossom again, at which point this ability refreshes.

NAME: NATURE’S PROTECTION
DESCRIPTION: A rowanwood wand woven with a single unicorn hair that glints different colors in the light, a ward against evil. While this wand is active, you are immune to hexes.

NAME: WINTRY WILL
DESCRIPTION: A delicate holly wand with an enchanted unmelting ice crystal set near the tip. While this wand is active, you are immune to cold, and you can use an action (AP1) to chill the air around you.

NAME: FARLAY’S WALL
DESCRIPTION: A solid elderwood wand engraved with glowing protective runes that can shield you from harm. While this wand is active, you can use it to cast Barrier with a roll of 18. Once you do this, the runes stop glowing for 24 hours, and you cannot use the ability again until they restore.

NAME: CELESTIAL HARMONY
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of ivory—ethically sourced, of course—with a silver tip that emits a soothing hum when used. Gives a +4 to Tact checks and can be used as an action (2AP) to calm down a creature. Roll Creature against its Concentration, and on success, you calm it down, although it will likely still be wary and ready to become aggressive again at a provocation.

NAME: EMERALD ENVY
DESCRIPTION: A jade wand wrapped in precious lunar silver filaments, with two elegant leaves embossed into its silver base. Gives a +2 to Herbalism checks, and allows you to cast the spell Language of the Earth without rolling for it, even if you don’t know the spell, taking one degree of success on the spell.

NAME: HEARTWOOD
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from passionoak, a rare red wood imbued with emotional power, said to be a symbol of love. Gives +4 to one Charm skill chosen at creation, and once per day, you can use it to soothe one non-Wound condition from somebody other than yourself. The wand must be active to use this ability.

NAME: ELEMENTALIST
DESCRIPTION: A silvered wand with a ring of small gemstones set along the end, representing the natural elements. Grants a +2 to Evocation, and you can expend one of the 6 gems to take an extra +4 to a given Evocation spell. The wand regains any spent gems at daybreak.

NAME: VOIDWEAVER
DESCRIPTION: An ebony wand coated in hellclaw sap and set with a small clouded purple crystal. You can use it to summon a Cloak of Darkness that settles over a place (2AP), only able to be penetrated by magic light. You can only have one Cloak of Darkness at a time. When inside your Cloak of Darkness, you can take on an appearance of darkness, rendering you effectively invisible unless targeted with magic light.

NAME: SPECTRAL WHISPER
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of transparent crystal that’s permanently cool to the touch. While this wand is active, you can take a +2 bonus to Necromancy, and you can see and speak to ghostly spirits that are otherwise invisible. When in a place of death, like a graveyard, you can use this wand to summon one (AP3), although it might not be happy to be summoned.

NAME: MARQUEZ’S BATTERY
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of clear crystal filled with a glowing blue liquid. The wand has 12 points of Surge, and you can spend as many as you like to add that number to a given spell check you make. The wand restores any spent points of Surge when you get a full night of sleep or take the Rest Downtime option.

NAME: THUNDERING ROAR
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from the bone of a giant beast, inlaid with its fangs at the tip, and a base cast in snatchknot resin. The wand can release a primal roar, giving a +6 to the Shatter spell, which you can cast whether you know it or not. You can increase the bonus to +10, but doing so damages the wand, reducing its condition by 100 materials.

NAME: ARHELM’S TIMEPIECE
DESCRIPTION: A burnished gold wand with an enchanted hourglass set near the tip. The wand gives +2 to Chronomancy, and you can use it (2AP) to give one of the following effects: • Give a target 3AP • Travel back in time as if using the spell Fabelyn’s Pen of Fate but only 15 seconds back • Let someone reroll any check once, but they must take the new roll • Gain an insight that you would be able to gain in 10 seconds • Slow time to 1/10th speed for you for 10 seconds (your time) • Once you’ve used any of these abilities, the hourglass flips, and takes one hour to empty, until which time you cannot use this ability again.

NAME: THE SEA KING’S WILL
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of seashell, decorated with pearls and woven with a single mermaid’s hair. While this wand is active, you can breathe underwater and swim in any direction at the same speed you can walk, and you can see a short distance underwater even in complete darkness.

NAME: STARLIGHT
DESCRIPTION: A wand coated in lunar silver with a pale blue crystal set at the tip. While this wand is active, you take a +2 to Illusion, and you can summon and control up to three Starlights, glowing points of light that move at your command to a point you can see. You can leave them in one place out of sight, but you cannot command them unless they are in sight, except to recall them to your wand. If you combine the three into one point, you can make a blinding light that makes people roll Concentration versus your Illusion or be forced to look away.

NAME: ELERY’S WAND OF STORMS
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from the blackened wood of a lightning-struck tree with a jagged quartz crystal embedded in the base that appears to crackle. While this wand is active, you take no penalties from the weather, you can add +4 to any spells using elemental lightning, and you can change the weather. When you do, point your wand to the sky and shout out the name of the weather you want, and a peal of thunder sounds as the weather changes in a very contained area around you for one hour. The quartz in the wand stops crackling once you do for 24 hours, and you cannot use this ability again until you do.

NAME: FROSTBITE
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of enchanted unmelting ice with a hollywood grip. While this wand is active, you are immune to cold, and you can manipulate any ice around you as if it were an extension of your body. You can unleash a flash of freezing energy that freezes water around you, but after using this ability, it must spend 12 hours recharging.

NAME: KRISTJÁNSDÓTTIR’S BLOODDRINKER
DESCRIPTION: A twisted blood-red wand made from the thorny branches of the snareleaf plant. While this wand is active, after making a spell check, you can take a Condition stack in exchange for replacing one of the dice of your choice with a 10. Improvement rolls are triggered with the original roll.

NAME: WINDRIDER
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from lightweight sycamore engraved with symbols representing the wind, flight, and freedom. While this wand is active, take a +2 to Agility, the wind around you acts as an extension of your body, and you can use this ability to glide and fall from any height unharmed. The wand also grants 10 minutes of unrestrained flight at your running speed, which you can use all at once or split up between different uses. Any used flight magic is restored when you get a full night of sleep or take the Rest Downtime option.

NAME: SCHOLAR’S SCEPTER
DESCRIPTION: An oversized ash wand with a dragon’s head carved into the end, wreathed with rare magical components. While active, this wand gives a +4 bonus to improvement and spell-learning rolls.

NAME: DREAMER’S KISS
DESCRIPTION: A thin wand made from ethereal moonstone and set with a pale pink crystal in the tip. While this wand is active, you take a +2 to Hypnomancy. In addition, you can hold it over a sleeping person to gain a vision of what they’re dreaming about, and you can use a magic charge to change their dream as if casting Dream Whispers with one degree of success. Once you have used this ability on someone, they must wake up and be asleep again before you can use it on them again.

NAME: IFRIT’S FIREWALKER
DESCRIPTION: A wand formed from an enchanted, unbreaking salt pillar formed around an ifrit’s chain. While this wand is active, you are immune to heat, and fire cannot hurt you. You can raise your temperature and return it to normal at will, and you can use this to force anybody who touches you to roll Endurance versus DC15 or your Evocation (your choice) or Resist Wound.

NAME: HERMES’ GIFT
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from white plate with solid gold trim and a pair of spectral white angels’ wings. While this wand is active, you can use it to telepathically deliver a message to any willing target you’ve seen in person before, just by touching the wand. The wand also gives a +4 to Athletics checks to run swiftly.

NAME: LYSANDER’S BLACKCALL
DESCRIPTION: A scorched-black wand made of coarse, burned feybough wood. Grants a +6 bonus to one spell, chosen at creation. It smells like burning fireplaces and Christmas spice, which is nice in December but makes you feel out of place in July.

NAME: LUNARLIGHT GIFT
DESCRIPTION: A wand of brushed cherrywood lined with thin stands of lunar silver filament. Whenever you cast a spell with this wand, you can choose to use up one of the lunar silver filaments on the wand, damaging this wand by 100 materials and increasing your degrees of success by 1. You can choose to use this ability even after seeing the results of the spell, but you cannot use it if your wand has less than 100 materials of condition remaining.

NAME: FABELYN’S EYE
DESCRIPTION: An intricate, ornate wand inlaid with small, finely detailed brass ornaments that realign themselves based on incomprehensible signs from the immediate future, and they align when danger is near. While this wand is active, you can choose to take 4AP at the start of Action instead of rolling. Your GM may also give you warnings that you’re approaching danger when you have this wand equipped.

NAME: MARWYN’S LAUGHTER
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of enchanted redwood, brushed with pixie dust. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Stealth checks, Illusion spells, and Demonology spells any time your Trouble rank is above 0. The great mage Marwyn liked to cause problems. This eventually got him killed, but hopefully you’ll fare better.

NAME: RAZOR INSTINCT
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of thick, knotted magic wood felled by lightning and carved roughly by hand, that grants a deep connection to primal magics. While this wand is equipped, you can sense the presence of any magic user within five meters, even through walls, except through antimagic barriers. This works even on targets who are invisible, under nondetection, or in adjacent planes. The wand is larger than most and easy to notice, though, so anyone trying to sneak away from you might notice the giant stick you’ve got and will know to stay away.

NAME: STARBURST
DESCRIPTION: A heavy, deeply ostentatious wand made of steel and feybough woven together in a volatile combination, with a brilliant crystal set in the end that lights up with the user’s competitive spirit. Grants a +4 bonus to all checks when the user is in a Duel. The drawback is that everyone knows you’re going to challenge them to a duel when you have this wand.

NAME: SAPHI’S SIGHT
DESCRIPTION: A delicate wand made of lightweight fairy yew that blossoms with tiny spectral flowers when near magic plants. When this wand is active, you take a +2 to Herbalism, and you automatically succeed on any checks to identify a magic plant, though the GM may still ask you to roll to determine something unusual, like if a plant is a unique hybrid or if there’s something wrong with it. The wand does not tell you if it’s a good idea to eat the plant or not, just what the plant is. Proceed at your own risk.

NAME: CLATHRA’S WALTZ
DESCRIPTION: A sleek black wand made of extraplanar materials that shifts slowly with its tail-like tip, searching out its surroundings. When this wand is active, you can teleport freely as if using the Blink spell, but only two meters at a time, to a point you can see, so long as there is nothing in between you and the point you intend to teleport to. If you attempt to blink and something is in your way, you collide with the object and come out of your teleport, which can be a rough outcome for everyone involved if you don’t realize someone is invisible between you and the target and you try to blink through them.

NAME: MONK’S PRAYER
DESCRIPTION: A simple staff made of cedar, brushed with silk-snag resin, and carved into a symbol at the end unique to each Monk’s Prayer. When this wand is active, you can spend an hour casting a spell to add one degree of success to the spell, possibly changing a failure to a success. If you cast the spell as a ritual, it takes this bonus automatically.

NAME: BARON’S BURST
DESCRIPTION: An ornate, detailed wand with a thick base wrapped in black gold leaf and fired with draconic flame. When this wand is active, your spells gain one degree of success on a critical success, as the ornate lines running along the wand light up with the surge of magic power.

NAME: JUMPING JACK
DESCRIPTION: A balsam wand half carved in one plane and half carved in another, the wand is long and thin and quivers in your hand, ready to bounce across the planes. When this wand is active, you can touch it to a point on the ground and speak an activation phrase. Any time afterwards, you can speak the activation phrase again and instantly teleport to the point where you first activated it. Once the wand teleports, it must recharge for 24 hours before it can teleport again.

NAME: KING’S SCEPTER
DESCRIPTION: An ornate, gaudy wand lined with brilliant, colorful magic materials. While this wand is active, you can place enchantments on objects without having to spend the materials to do so, although enchanting on object with an Advanced or higher spell will still cost the amount of the spell itself.

NAME: WITCHRUNNER
DESCRIPTION: A classic for the Witches’ Run—hence the unoriginal name—this simple lunar silver wand glows with a faint violet light from the tip. While this wand is active, you can run at twice your regular speed and jump up to twice your height, and grants a +2 to Evocation, Athletics and Agility. However, when using this ability, the wand lights up with a brilliant glow and your steps sound like small lightning claps, so you won’t get anywhere stealthily.

NAME: CURSESTONE
DESCRIPTION: A wand with a cold stone grip inlaid with dreadful sigils. While this wand is active, you can make a DC12 Resist roll to force somebody you can see to Resist a non-Wound condition of your choice, DC12. This is widely considered rude, though.

NAME: STEELSKIN
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of steelnose scales, always warm to the touch. While this wand is active, you can make a DC14 Endurance check, activating the wand’s inherent magic on success and tapping into a draconic transformation. You can transform yourself and one other person for each degree of success past the first for five minutes. While transformed, you are covered with a thin sheen of silvery scales that are warm to the touch, and you are immune to heat and can take a +10 to Endurance rolls, and Evocation spells cannot force you to Resist (except a backfire from your own). This move can backfire like a Standard spell.

NAME: MOCKING WHISPERS
DESCRIPTION: A beautiful whisperwood wand set with rose gold and carved symbols, it feels like it shimmers in your hand. While this wand is active, you can perfectly mimic any voice you’ve heard before. The wand will guide you to mimic that person’s speech patterns and vocal mannerisms as well, but it isn’t a guarantee: anyone who knows the mimicked person well can roll Read Person versus your Deception + 8, figuring out it’s a fake on success.

NAME: ELERY’S HAND
DESCRIPTION: A wand that doesn’t look like one, more like a brace fitted over the back of the hand and lined with spellsteel bands. While this wand is active, you can roll Evocation + 6 instead of Athletics to move objects you can touch, although you may lack finesse, and you can clench the hand and roll Evocation, DC15, creating a shockwave on success. Anyone hit by the shockwave must roll Endurance versus your spell check or be knocked down. This move can backfire like a Standard spell.

NAME: THE MAW
DESCRIPTION: A knotted hellclaw wand with a coarse grip that digs into your flesh if you grip it too hard, The Maw is a popular wand that feels like it becomes “a part of you” in a way that puts some people off. While this wand is active, you can take a +6 to all checks to metabolize potions. When you unequip this wand, you must Resist Loss, DC5, as it feels like pulling a part of yourself away.

NAME: LI HUAN’S BELL
DESCRIPTION: A pale silver wand with a fey crystal on the end that chimes like a small, soft bell. When this wand is active, you can roll Charm, DC5 at the end of any Downtime block, including sleeping at night. For each degree of success, you take one Blessing. Any time someone you can see is about to make a check, you can spend a Blessing by chiming the crystal at them. If you do, they take an extra degree of success on their check.

NAME: BLACKBANE
DESCRIPTION: A hooked wand made of hellclaw and white-tipped seeker braided together and burned until black. While this wand is active, you can declare someone a Nemesis. When you have a declared Nemesis, you can spend half an hour with the wand to roll Tracking, DC5 and get one clue about their location per degree of success. You can take a +4 bonus to any roll that would cause your Nemesis to Resist. Once you have declared a Nemesis, you cannot declare a new one until you have unequipped this wand and equipped it again, at which point it loses its connection to the previous Nemesis, or until the Nemesis has been Downed.

NAME: GOLDEN NEXUS
DESCRIPTION: A heavy, brassy wand with a shimmering crystalline core in the tip that exudes deep, powerful magic. When this wand is active, Advanced spells only cost 300 materials to cast.

NAME: STAFF OF THE SCRIBE
DESCRIPTION: A large but simple wand, with a long oak body and a metal sculpture of an owl’s head on the end. While this wand is active, any rune with 4 or more glyphs takes half the time to create.

NAME: ENCHANTING BREATH
DESCRIPTION: A delicate fairywood wand in pale lavender that seems to always shimmer with a magical, spectral dust. While this wand is active, any time you succeed to enchant an object, you can choose to make the enchantment permanent. Many people are disappointed by this wand, thinking it makes their breath smell good. Just lay off the garlic instead.

NAME: BRUTE
DESCRIPTION: A deceptively delicate wand with a long, thin design with a lumini feather in the grip. While this wand is active, you can take a +2 to Athletics and Endurance. You can take an extra +6 to any Athletics or Endurance check, but doing so decreases the wand’s condition by 100 materials.

NAME: VALEY’S SIREN
DESCRIPTION: A bright-red wand made of whisperwood, brushed with hellclaw sap and coated with powdered red draconic scales. While this wand is active, you cannot be detected with divination. If somebody attempts to cast a Divination spell that would detect you, it fails, and the wand alerts you psychically that somebody attempted it. The only problem is that it psychically warns you with what it thinks is a good sound to use, and sometimes it picks a bad one.

NAME: GREEDRAT
DESCRIPTION: A simple wand made of mahogany with a rat head carved at the end, with feylight crystals for eyes. While this wand is active, you can spend an hour touching upon its magic and roll Search, DC10, taking 100 materials per degree of success as it coalesces materials from across the interplanar continuum. You must Resist when you use this ability, DC10.

NAME: AELYN’S ARRAY
DESCRIPTION: An intricate wand with a pure white crystal body and a deep violet grip that turns into a spectral, glowing array of starlight when held by its owner, this crystallomantic wand was only recently invented and has taken the world by storm, mostly because it’s pretty. While attuned to this wand, you can add 2 degrees of success to any spell to align with a type of crystal. All the magic influencers on TikTok have one.

NAME: DEVIL’S THRESHER
DESCRIPTION: A wand that looks a lot scarier than it is, with a jagged red body and a split at the end like a jaw lined with knifelike quartz “teeth,” it thirsts for ripping and tearing, but just, like, plants. While this wand is active, you automatically succeed on any check to harvest a magic plant at or under 1000 materials, as long as you’ve identified the plant.

NAME: EMBERWALL
DESCRIPTION: An elegant white wand set with a piece of emberhorn antler, this wand emits a calming, soothing sensation. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to any spell backfire, but the wand takes 50 materials’ worth of damage when you do, as the antler bone is slowly spent. If you fail a spell backfire, you can choose to take 250 materials’ worth of damage to the wand instead of taking a Condition stack.

NAME: CLOUDLESS’ BASTION
DESCRIPTION: A large, heavy wand with a thick, banded design and a single water wyrm scale at the base of a small cage at the tip of the wand, which captures magic energy and returns it, allowing practice without expending as many materials. While this wand is active, the material cost to learn a new spell is cut in half.

NAME: DREAMKISS
DESCRIPTION: A deeply detailed and beautifully designed wand with a feylight crystal attached by a shimmery pink ribbon. While this wand is active, every time you go to sleep, you can cast any Advanced or lower spell you know as a Basic spell, and the spell will be cast slowly over the course of a night of sleep. You can’t know ahead of time when the spell effect will take hold, just that it will be sometime while you sleep, so maybe think twice about setting up a Greater Warp while you sleep.

NAME: NALIA’S QUILL
DESCRIPTION: A wand made with a griffin’s feather that glows golden while casting spells or creating runes. While this wand is active, the material cost of any rune is reduced by 25 per glyph. This cannot reduce the cost of a rune below zero.

NAME: ARGON’S AUTHORITY
DESCRIPTION: A regal staff made with shed wyvern claw, it exudes a powerful artificy energy. While this wand is equipped, you can take a +8 to attune to new artifacts, and a +4 to Resist checks from failing an attunement roll.

NAME: BELHAM’S BLACKOUT
DESCRIPTION: A long, thin wand brushed with hellclaw sap and pixie dust, giving it a darkly twinkling look. When this wand is active, you can touch it to a rune, disabling that rune for 30 minutes. You cannot use this ability again until the 30 minutes have run out.

NAME: MAELSTROM
DESCRIPTION: A simple, plain wand made of enchanted oak that vibrates with a faint magical energy. While this wand is active, you can tap into its magic and let out a flow of intense, shuddering magic energy for 5 minutes, during which time you can take a +6 to cast any spell. At the end of this time, you must Resist, DC20.

NAME: HENRY
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of ashwood, with a sprig of bright-red Nephera’s Envy at the tip. While this wand is active, you can summon a small figure, which the wand creator demanded under the patent must always be called “Henry,” to do your bidding. Henry is an extraplanar creature in the shape of a blob a few inches tall, made of a simple gelatinous material, and will follow your psychic commands. It has 0 Resolve and disappears if it would ever have to Resist, and rolls 0/4, and can fit through any shape water can pass through. If Henry is forced to Resist and disappears, you must wait 24 hours before you can summon Henry again. Most people dismiss the wand and Henry as strange, but then end up very attached to the little guy and agree he’s definitely a Henry.

NAME: LADY OF ARSENALS
DESCRIPTION: A short, ornate scepter inlaid with artificers’ runes. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to any checks to use artifacts, including on backfires.

NAME: BLACKBLOOD PROMISE
DESCRIPTION: A simple wand inlaid with black quartz teeming with demonological enchantments, its faces seeming to reflect the viewer in different moods. While this wand is active, you can choose to take a +6 on any Persuasion, Tact, or Win Over roll, but if you fail the roll, the target will be aware that you tried to influence their mind with magic, and will react appropriately. And they’re typically not super appreciative.

NAME: GARLYTH’S SECRET VESSEL
DESCRIPTION: A small wand with a brassy figure like a cage at the end that has a magical fluid suspended in it. While this wand is active, you can use this fluid in place of 100 materials in brewing a potion, which empties the wand even if you used it on a potion with less than 100 material cost. The wand regains its magic in six hours.

NAME: BRASS GAMBIT
DESCRIPTION: A dark brass wand said to be linked to the lingering consciousness of great strategists and generals through history. It’s probably a lie, but it’s still a pretty good wand. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to any check made during Downtime.

NAME: VIAL ARMY
DESCRIPTION: A wand with a canister in the base set with potion vials that gleam with shimmering, spectral magic. While this wand is active, any time you would brew one potion of 100 material cost or lower, you instead obtain two, without spending extra materials. Every self-respecting vial girl has one of these around somewhere. Usually decked with stickers.

NAME: WORD OF WRATH
DESCRIPTION: A wand with a twisting spiral design, black onyx forming outwards to a jagged tip. While this wand is active, you can create a blast of force from the tip as if casting the Shatter spell with a roll of 15. It cannot hurt creatures, but it destroys any object that would be destroyed with a Shatter roll of 15. Starfall staff would like to ask you not to use this wand. Especially the cleaning staff.

NAME: EYES OF THE ETHER
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of extraplanar material that slowly shifts and rotates in your hand. While this wand is active, you can choose to open your mind at any point to a telepathic communication with a specific extraplanar creature chosen at the time of creating this wand. The extraplanar creature must consent to being linked to the wand and will generally be friendly, but if this wand originally belonged to somebody else, there’s no guarantee the associated creature will like its new host. You can cast the spell Planar Communion at will to communicate with the associated creature, and if the creature is friendly to you, you can summon it without rolling. Even if it is friendly to you, though, it will have its own motivations completely alien to yours, and will often have incomprehensible ulterior motives.

NAME: DARKSPARK
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from black enchanted fairywood that is straight, but appears to be crooked in a different way depending on what angle it’s viewed from. While this wand is active, you can roll Persuasion or Deception, +4, DC10, to incite a sudden rush of anger in a person you can see. The target must roll Willpower versus your check or become anywhere from annoyed to incoherent with rage depending on your degrees of success. If they succeed on their Willpower check with two or more degrees of success, they know they were being manipulated, and on three or more, they know it was you.

NAME: HEAVEN’S BALANCE
DESCRIPTION: A shimmering white wand steeped with fey magic, this wand feels soothing to the touch. While this wand is active, you can align with one more type of crystal at once than otherwise. If you align with the same crystal twice, add the degrees of success on that effect. It also allows you to align with cheeseburgers. This doesn’t do anything, and nobody knows why it’s a thing. No, you can’t align with hamburgers. These things happen when you involve fey magic.

NAME: STEEL-CLAD VERDICT
DESCRIPTION: A jet-black wand with a piece of a magic item retrieved from the body of a black worm. While this wand is active, you always know when you’re perceiving an illusion. You still have to roll to determine the true nature of what’s behind the illusion, but you can take a +4 on the check to do so.

NAME: NEPHERA’S LIGHT
DESCRIPTION: A deep jade-green wand with a dried snagweed vine wrapped around it. While this wand is active, you can cultivate an extra 500 materials’ worth of magic plants. If you unequip this wand and end up with more plants than you have capacity for, you have 24 hours to get rid of them or reequip this wand, or the plants die.

NAME: BUILDER’S BALLAD
DESCRIPTION: A finely engineered metal wand in royal blue with wandcrafting runes around the tip. While this wand is active, you can add a +6 to any checks to craft or repair wands, and you never suffer a penalty for making a wand for someone you don’t know well.

NAME: DELAIR’S MERCY
DESCRIPTION: A wand inlaid with lunar silver and fey quartz, and a thick, brassy grip that lights up with magic as it absorbs spent materials. While this wand is active, failing an Advanced spell instead returns all your materials, and failing a Hex instead returns half. Please don’t take that as your encouragement to cast hexes, though. Delair didn’t mean to make it do that and specifically put out a statement saying “please don’t cast hexes.”

NAME: TOUCH OF THE MUSE
DESCRIPTION: A braided fairywood wand woven with a single fey hair, teeming with a creative energy. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Art, and any time you roll Art on Downtime, you can take an additional +4. There’s been more than one case of a great artist succeeding well enough to leave this wand behind and get a flashy expensive one instead and then no longer making good art. It’s kind of sad.

NAME: STARFORGED
DESCRIPTION: An ornate wand made from starsteel, a sparkling alloy made with a rare cosmic metal. While this wand is active, you can manipulate gravity, allowing you to cast Levitate at will without rolling for it. Take a 20 for the check.

NAME: MINDSEEKER
DESCRIPTION: A wand carved out of a single amethyst formation, set into an ornate black steel grip embossed with eye symbols. While this wand is active, you can attempt to overpower a person’s mind. Give a short, simple command. A person must succeed on a DC20 Willpower check or follow your command. They must follow the command by the letter, but they can choose how within those confines. You can use this ability once per day.

NAME: DRAGON’S FURY
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from dragon bone with a blood-red crystal shaped to look like a dragon’s eye at the end. While this wand is active, you have a +4 to all Evocation spells, and you can cast Dragonbreath at will without rolling for it, taking a 20 on the check.

NAME: KALON’S CRYSTAL PRISON
DESCRIPTION: A cold steel wand with bars over a purple crystal. While this wand is active, you can cast Steal Soul at any time without rolling for it. Take a 25 on the check. If you choose to store the stolen soul in the wand, it will remain there indefinitely, giving a +4 to all Necromancy checks until you use the soul. You cannot use this ability again until the soul is removed from the wand, and no more than once per day.

NAME: LUNAR DANCE
DESCRIPTION: A shimmering platinum wand with a precious illusion stone embedded in a moonstone crescent moon on the end. While this wand is active, you can take a +6 to all Stealth checks at night, as the magic of the wand obscures you. You can use the wand to cast Invisibility without rolling for it, taking a 20 on the check, but the illusion stone becomes a black stone for 24 hours afterwards, during which time the +6 Stealth bonus is lost.

NAME: RADIANT DAWN
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from untrimmed dawnbark, a pure white enchanted tree that glows faintly by starlight, woven with unicorn hairs. While this wand is active, you have a +4 to Restoration spells, and you can cast Saint’s Touch at will, taking a 20 on the check. You can use the wand’s magic to ignore one stack of Doubt you would otherwise take, but the wand loses its abilities for 6 hours after you do so.

NAME: WITCHHUNTER
DESCRIPTION: A jagged wand made from enchanted ebony and adorned with eagle feathers. While this wand is active, you can use it to track down a given magic object or phenomenon, or track down its source. Describe what you want to track down, and this becomes your Target. You have a +4 on all checks to locate your Target, and you can cast Visions at will, taking a 15 on the check to receive answers related to tracking your Target. You cannot change your Target until you have found it.

NAME: ABERLON’S PHOENIX HEART
DESCRIPTION: A wand carved from a tree burned by sacred phoenix fire and adorned with a single phoenix feather. While this wand is active, you can control any fire nearby as if it is an extension of your body, and you are immune to heat and fire. In addition, if an effect would ever Down you, you can choose to activate Rebirth, which covers you in a veil of sacred fire and removes all your Condition stacks. If you do so, the phoenix feather on this wand will be scorched, and the wand will be unusable for a full month while it regrows.

NAME: MASTER OF THE WATERY KINGDOM
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from ocean-floor bedrock and inlaid with an enchanted pearl that glows with a sea-green light. While this wand is active, you can breathe underwater, and water around you moves as if an extension of your body. If you rest while underwater, you can restore 2 Conditions for every 1 you would normally restore.

NAME: STAFF OF THE MASTER
DESCRIPTION: A long, heavy staff made of tangled dreamglow yew, a precious magic tree that only grows deep in fey enclaves, teeming with deep, powerful insight. While this wand is active, your spells trigger improvement rolls also on any roll with 1 degree of failure.

NAME: STORMING BLESSING
DESCRIPTION: A wand with a thick vein of lunar silver, spiraling from the base to a storm crystal set in the tip, which crackles with a wild magic energy. While this wand is active, you can choose one Stat at the end of any Downtime block including sleeping at night, and take a +3 bonus to that Stat. You can change the Stat that takes the bonus at any time as well, by making a DC22 Concentration roll, calling on chaotic storming magic. On a failure or a backfire, the magic explodes, and you must Resist, DC16, although if you backfire on a success, you still successfully change the Stat that receives the bonus.

NAME: HEAVEN’S JUDGMENT
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of white celestial emerald, a precious and rare material said to be a leftover remnant from the creation of magic and to be linked to tychomantic fate. While this wand is active, any time someone you can see makes a Resist roll, you can force it to fail. When you do, you must Resist at the same DC.

NAME: PLANESTRIDER
DESCRIPTION: A long, gnarled staff made of weaverweb, a tree that grows in SP-TT10229, a plane on the border of open ether, imbued with interplanar energies. While this wand is active, it grants a +2 to Summoning and to Teleportation, and you can split it down the middle into two smaller wands, which casts Plane Walk with a roll of 19. You can rejoin the two halves into one, which instantly returns you to your home plane. If you lose one or both halves of the wand while you are on another plane, though, you will need to cast Plane Walk to return. Once you have used the wand’s ability to cast Plane Walk, you must wait 24 hours for the ability to recharge again. The quintessential planehopper’s wand, and the primary reason planehoppers get lost and never found again. Hey, they’ve been warned.

NAME: DRAGON’S INTELLECT
DESCRIPTION: A wand carved from a dragon’s fang and imbued with a sliver of the dragon’s keen mind. While this wand is active, you automatically succeed on all spell-learning checks.

NAME: MEDITATIVE HEART
DESCRIPTION: A wand carved from dreamglow yew and set with a single dreamglow blossom set in amber resin, it shimmers with a faint pink light, small motes of spectral light that swirl around the tip. While this wand is active, you can perform any Downtime activity (other than Rest) in a Dreamglow Trance, which lets you gain the benefits of the Rest option along with whatever option you choose.

NAME: SKULLMASON
DESCRIPTION: A thin, delicate wand made from a small sliver of a stone giant’s bone. While this wand is active, you can cast Amalgamate as a Basic spell. You cannot have more than 5 amalgamated creatures at a time with this. Good if you want a skeletal army, which necromancers are all inexplicably fascinated by.

NAME: ARCHITECT OF GLYPHS
DESCRIPTION: A deceptively simple wand made of dark wood in a classic shape set in a base of solid glyphburn, a magic metal that looks like deep gold and carries strong runological magic. While this wand is active, take a +4 bonus to Runology, and you can create a rune of 6 or more glyphs while treating three glyphs of your choice as 0 intensity rank and 0 material cost.

NAME: SACRED INKWELL
DESCRIPTION: An elegant wand made from hellclaw inlaid with metal forged from a steelnose carapace. While this wand is active, you can take a +3 to Alchemy, and you can unscrew the sigil-shaped engraved head and pull out a long needle-like dropper, which you can dip into a potion of 250 material cost or less. The wand will consume the potion, but once it has, you can use the dropper to fill a vial with a copy of the potion once every 24 hours. You can “feed” the wand a new potion at any time to change which potion it provides, but it can only contain one at a time.

NAME: VYLA’S EYE
DESCRIPTION: A small, thin staff made of hypnotic pearl, a magic compound rich with hypnomantic energy found only in deep sea caves. While this wand is active, every time you go to sleep for a full night of rest, you can cast the spell Dreamwalk without spending any material components on one person who you saw in person since the last time you slept. The beautiful pearlescent shimmer of the wand is the symbol of dreamcreeps, weird hypnomancers who pry on people’s dreams, and is thus a little unpopular. Except with dreamcreeps. They love it.

NAME: MARWYN’S MOCKERY
DESCRIPTION: A brass banded wand made from 38 interlaid bands, each one forged under a slightly different type of magic. While this wand is active, any time you see a spell cast, you can roll Comprehend or Analyze versus the spell check. On success, that spell becomes stored in the wand, and you can cast it at the same roll it was originally cast with. If the spell is Advanced or higher, you will still need to provide the material cost. You can only have one spell stored this way at a time. While you have a spell stored, you can take a +2 bonus to whatever field the spell is in.

NAME: JUNKPILE
DESCRIPTION: A wand with, uh, eclectic style, made of about sixty different interesting magical materials fused together, coming out with a magical propensity for, well, collecting random junk. While this wand is active, any time materials are used up nearby (excluding trades, only situations where the materials no longer exist), you obtain half the materials used, up to a maximum of 250. Does it make carrying this ugly thing around worth it, though?

NAME: SONGWON’S RIGHTEOUS FURY
DESCRIPTION: A jagged, dangerous-looking wand made from dragon’s claws, crackling with sparking energy. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Evocation, and you can fire bolts of lightning from the tip of your wand. Roll Evocation, DC20, and on success, a target must roll Agility versus your check or be forced to Resist. This move can backfire like a Standard spell. You can also roll Evocation, DC20 to launch yourself forward with a clap of lightning, moving to a point you can see in an instant and arriving with a thunderous explosion, although if you fail or only have one degree of success, you must Resist. Songwon perhaps burned the brightest of all history’s evokers, but she had the bad habit of proving herself with fights against dragons, so she didn’t leave as many magic legacies as she could have if she weren’t stupid.

NAME: MOONSTRIDER
DESCRIPTION: A pale, silvery wand formed of a naturally fallen branch from the elusive lunar birch tree, glowing with soft moonlight rich with illusory energies. While this wand is active, you can create an illusory double of yourself, as in the spell Second Self, except that the illusory double is able to go anywhere and not just where you can see, and can follow more abstract instructions and perform basic reasoning. You can also create any number of illusory doubles with this ability. The wand has 2 hours of this ability, and it is split between each illusory double at any given point, so that one double lasts for 2 hours, four doubles last for half an hour, and so on. You can summon and dispel these doubles at will, and the 2-hour charge refreshes every morning at the first light of daybreak.

NAME: CRAFTER’S GRACE
DESCRIPTION: An intricate wand made with wood from a tree blessed by a unicorn. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Wandcrafting, and you can repair wand damage up to 300 materials’ worth instantly, only paying half the materials spent in the repair. The material-cost bonus does not apply to regular wand-repair rolls.

NAME: REDMARE’S VEIL
DESCRIPTION: A deep violet wand woven with a dried vine of the eternity bell, a plant rich with chronomancy that’s only been found in the Amazon. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Chronomancy, and you can activate the wand’s magic to stop time, as if casting the Freeze spell with 6 degrees of success. Once you use this ability, the vine wrapped around the wand darkens to black, and the wand loses its abilities for 6 hours while the vine slowly regains its color.

NAME: HUNGRY WARTCAT
DESCRIPTION: A thick, sturdy wand made from broken-heart oak, a cursed and evil plant, but the wand’s not evil, don’t worry. Just a little greedy. While this wand is active, you can metabolize two potions at once. Under the official patent, the wand is called “Hungry Wartcat, Nom Nom Nom,” but, uh, nobody calls it that.

NAME: BASKET OF BLOSSOMS
DESCRIPTION: A wand made from woven strands of fairywood, dreamglow yew, and passionoak, braided with snag silk. The wand feels bursting with vibrant natural magics just being close to it, and it smells like the forest. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Herbalism, and your cultivation capacity for magic plants increases by 500. In addition, you can bring one magic plant with you, up to 500 materials’ worth, stored inside an interplanar pocket within the wand and bursting out from the wand on command. If you unequip this wand and are cultivating magic plants over your capacity, you have 24 hours to get rid of them or reequip this wand, or the extra plants will die.

NAME: PRINCESS ENCHANTRESS
DESCRIPTION: A beautiful, ornate wand with a black and red design that evokes a gothic style, carved in blackened whisperwood and brushed with snareleaf resin, giving it a blood-like sheen. While this wand is active, you can enchant yourself like you would an object, as the magic materializes in your blood. Keep in mind most enchantments are not a good idea to cast on a person.

NAME: ARGON’S COMMANDMENT
DESCRIPTION: A curled wand made with passionoak coldly tempered with enchanted black quartz, exuding a powerful, commanding aura. While this wand is active, you can use artifacts you are not attuned to, but you take a -4 penalty on checks to use them and automatically fail backfire checks with them. Artifacts tend to find this rude, though. Generally they can’t speak, so you usually won’t know.

NAME: LILIBEL’S LOOKOUT
DESCRIPTION: An ethereal white wand made of a naturally fallen whisperwood branch, brushed with sweet wishrose resin and dusted with pixie dust. It appears to hum in a resonant tone when close by. While this wand is active, you can take a +2 to Divination, and you can cast Heaven’s Lens as a Basic spell. Once you’ve cast Heaven’s Lens, you can attempt a DC24 Divination roll to hide the porthole, but if you fail, you cannot try again on the same porthole. A powerful divinatory wand, but many men avoid using it because it’s “too girly.” Lilibel has been reported as saying “that’s okay.”

NAME: NECROTIC EYE OF ROT
DESCRIPTION: A wildly over-the-top wand made of griffin bone, carved at the end to look like a human skull, with a magically-bound troll eye in its jaw. While this wand is active, you can take a +2 to Necromancy, and you can cast Voices from Beyond as a Basic spell, although you can only cast it once this way for any given creature you’re communicating with. It’s not exactly the cutest wand. Even the gothic aesthetic boards on Pinterest avoid it because it’s just a little much, you know?

NAME: HEART-EYED WHISPERER
DESCRIPTION: A classically simple wand made of black stardust birch, set in a demonsteel base engraved with the name of a true demon. While this wand is active, you can take a +2 to Demonology and a +2 to Persuasion, and if you’re able to hold a conversation with someone for one minute, keeping your voice in a low, even tone, you can roll Persuasion + 12 versus their Comprehend. On success, you can convince them of one thing per degree of success that they will believe is the absolute truth for one minute, or you can “spend” three degrees of success to have the target believe something for ten minutes. Once the effect wears off, they realize you affected them psychically, and unless they’re into that, they probably won’t be happy. This wand is one of the main reasons people don’t like demonologists.

NAME: BEASTBLOOD
DESCRIPTION: A wand carved of a single piece of griffin yew, an ancient tree that naturally drives powerful creatures nearby to territorial battle rages, set with a small trophy from a creature up to 5 Resolve, chosen at creation. While this wand is active, you can take a +2 to Bestiology, and you can activate the wand (2AP) to transfigure into a half-human version of the type of beast with a trophy set in the wand. You can stay transformed for up to 10 minutes or end the transformation early, and while transformed, you can roll with the creature’s Strong roll on anything the creature itself would roll Strong on as well. When your transformation ends, you must Resist, DC18, and cannot transform this way again for an hour.

NAME: KEY TO THE INNER WORLD
DESCRIPTION: A scepter made of fine lunar silver forged in the heat of dreamglow fire and smelted with pixie dust, with an ornate bell design at the end. When you first equip this wand, it envelops you in a wave of shimmering pink light and pulls you into a dreamscape for one hour. Roll Comprehend, DC28, and on a failure, the wand rejects you, and you must Resist, DC20, and you fail to equip the wand. On a success, you become the master of the dreamscape, activating the wand. While this wand is active, take a +4 to Hypnomancy, and you can cast Dreamworld as a Basic spell to interact with this dreamscape. If you use the spell Vyla’s Room, you can have it take place inside your dreamscape, and up to four people in the dreamscape including yourself can take advantage of it at once. You have this wand inside your dreamscape, too, and if you unequip this wand inside your dreamscape, the dreamscape is shattered and everyone in it wakes up and must Resist, DC18.

NAME: SILVER-EYED DRINKER OF PAIN
DESCRIPTION: A thick, black scepter carved from the raw core of the bleeding-heart oak, a deceptively simple design with a wooden body and a crow’s head engraved in the end. When you first equip this wand, the crow’s eyes light up in silver flame, and it emits a noxious black cloud, and you must Resist, DC19, five times in sequence as the toxic cloud attempts to force you into submission. If you reach 0 Resolve, the wand rejects you, and you fail to equip it. If you withstand it, the wand accepts you, and any time the wand is active, you can draw from its raw energy to power your magic, spending 500 materials’ worth of the wand’s condition to add one degree of success to a spell you cast, up to a maximum of 5 additional degrees of success at once. The wood becomes withered and cracked and frayed, and pieces begin to splinter off of the crow’s head, as its condition drops lower. When it is not at full condition, you can point the crow’s head towards another wand equipped by somebody you can see, and send a cloud of black magic that envelops the target wand (2AP). Roll Wandcrafting + 8 versus the target’s Endurance, and steal 500 materials’ worth of condition from the target’s wand per degree of success, adding it to this wand’s condition as the crow’s eyes light up in silver flame, drinking the target wand’s energy.

NAME: HOPE’S FINAL BEACON
DESCRIPTION: A small wand made of whisperwood heart crafted into a classic design, with a lunar platinum base woven with six unicorn hairs in a classic knot pattern. When you first equip this wand, it will test your spirit, lighting up with chronomantic energy as it throws you through a simulated version of the timestream, replaying different moments of history and of your life to see how you react. Roll Willpower, Perception, Improvise, Persuasion, and Endurance, all DC22, to pass its trials. Add your degrees of success and subtract your degrees of failure, and if the result is 3 or greater, the wand accepts you. If you fail, the wand rejects you, and you must Resist, DC20, and must wait 24 hours before you can attempt the trials again. While this wand is active, it glows with a pale brassy light, and you can take a +4 to Chronomancy. At the end of each day, you can activate its power to revisit one ten-minute block of time of your choice through the day, returning to the situation you were in at that point but with any knowledge and new skills you’ve acquired since then. Anything you do differently during this time will rewrite the course of the day, although the timestream will adjust itself to accommodate for your changes with the smallest overall change possible.

NAME: DANCE OF THE FEY PROPHETS
DESCRIPTION: A wand forged of a single piece of feylight crystal, set in a grip made of wood from a sleeping willow that a unicorn has slept under. When you first equip this wand, it takes you lurching between planes. Roll Comprehend, DC25, and on success, you link yourself to the wand and flow with it through the planes, becoming one with its wild fey magics, successfully equipping it. On a failure, you must Resist, DC20, and you are teleported to a location up to one kilometer away that will cause you trouble. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Teleportation, and you can cast the Greater Warp and Blink spells as Standard (Ritual) and Basic spells, respectively. In addition, fey are more likely to like you, and any magic effect that would read your thoughts or detect if you are lying will fail and instead force the person using them on you to Resist, DC18. Side effects include sometimes coming out of a teleportation with a funny hat on your head. The fey are just like that.

NAME: STAFF OF THE ARCHITECTS
DESCRIPTION: A long, elegant staff made of wood from the wyvern knot tree, a legendary rare plant only found deep in the Amazon and said to be the source of power for the Architects, ancient runic masters from the Amazonian floodplains. The countless fine engravings of runes along the length of the staff shift every time it’s used, and it is said that the staff is weaving an epic story over a thousand years with its runes. When you first equip this wand, it will test your will: while this wand is equipped, you cannot use any magic other than Runology, and you automatically fail any Resist roll. If you make it one week without reaching 0 Resolve or unequipping the wand, the wand will accept you, and it will become active. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Runology, and you can have two Runic Blessings active at once. In addition, any time a rune would cost less than 200 materials and take less than 2 hours to create, you can instead place it in ten minutes with 0 material cost.

NAME: THE TEN THOUSAND FACES OF THE DARK LADY
DESCRIPTION: A twisted black wand made from passionoak corrupted by demonic energy, woven with crimson hairs from the mane of a Nightmare. When you first equip this wand, it wrenches your consciousness into a realm of pure demonic energy, swirling with demonic contracts and cold logic. Roll Analyze, DC30, and on a failure, you must Resist Doubt, DC22. If failing this Resist roll puts you at 0 Resolve or 3 Doubt, you will be possessed by a demon for 24 hours. If you succeed, the wand links up to your mind, and you can hear its cold, calculating analysis as whispers in your head. While this wand is active, take a +2 to Logic, and you can cast the spell Spectral Mask as a Standard (Ritual) spell. You can also choose to roll the spell with Demonology instead of Illusion. You can tap into the calculating logic of the demonic mind at any point, increasing the Logic bonus to +6 for thirty minutes, but you must Resist Doubt, DC20 at the end of this period, and if this puts you at 0 Resolve or 3 Doubt, a demon will possess you for 24 hours.

NAME: MASTER OF THE CRYSTAL MATRIX
DESCRIPTION: A wand forged from the scales of a crystal dragon, glistening a thousand different colors depending on what angle it’s viewed from. When you first equip this wand, it envelops you in a spectral realm of endless crystalline alignments, swirling around you and threatening to drive you to madness. Roll Concentration, DC30, and on success, you successfully equip the wand as the alignment snaps into place around you and you feel the endless spectral realm of crystals become one with your consciousness. If you fail, you are knocked unconscious for 4 hours. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Crystallomancy, and you can align with an unlimited number of crystals, but you cannot stack the same alignment multiple times this way. If you backfire on any alignment roll, the matrix is disturbed, and you lose all your previous alignments.

NAME: THE DRAGON LORD’S STANDARD
DESCRIPTION: A staff forged from the willingly shed claw of a true dragon, set into a body of stardust birch, it radiates with a spectral energy that creates a shimmering trail behind it when it moves, with a commanding presence. When you first equip this wand, it battles you psychically for dominance. Roll Willpower, DC30, and on success, you project your will onto the wand and gain its respect, and it becomes active. If you fail, you must Resist, DC14, but each time you fail again, the Resist DC increases by 4. While this wand is active, you can take a +6 to any Persuasion or Win Over checks that come from a place of projecting authority or leadership or to interact with a dragon, and you can activate the wand to put out a call that the closest wyvern to you will hear regardless of distance. If the wyvern respects your authority, it will come to you and behave as a friendly creature, and if it does not, you can roll Persuasion with this wand’s +6 bonus versus the wyvern’s Strong roll, gaining its respect on a success. If you’ve called a wyvern with this ability and it is friendly to you, you can choose to mark that wyvern with your standard, and any time you activate this special ability, you can choose to call a specific wyvern you’ve marked instead of the closest wyvern. Keep in mind that calling a wyvern does not, in fact, solve all your problems. Sometimes you just have to own up to the fact that you spilled someone’s drink.

NAME: LUNAR MIST’S BLUE-EYED CHILD
DESCRIPTION: A wand carved from an entire white stardust birch sapling and crowned with a single Saphi’s whisperbloom, shimmering with an ethereal illusion energy. When you first equip this wand, roll Concentration, DC24. On success, the wand becomes active, and it creates a magical illusory copy of you that looks exactly the same as you except for radiant pale-blue eyes. While this wand is active, you can take a +4 to Illusion, and the second self created acts as a separate entity that has 5 Resolve and the exact same abilities as you and can mimic you and your mannerisms perfectly, but is a separate entity with its own intelligence, and is physically corporeal and capable of using magic. The second self is perfectly loyal to you, friendly and willing to do anything you ask it to, although it may have its particular personality quirks. If your second self reaches 0 Resolve, it disintegrates into a shower of moonlight, and this wand loses its abilities for 24 hours, at the end of which it recreates the second self with 1 Resolve restored. If you fail the check to activate the wand, you are instead locked in a parallel realm for 24 hours while the spectral copy is summoned in your place and causes mischief, which might get you in trouble. Possibly a lot of it.

NAME: THE FORGOTTEN FACELESS GOD
DESCRIPTION: A delicate, subtly twisted wand made of sleeping willow wood, set with a single weeping goddess crystal, a magic stone of mysterious origins said to be the frozen tear of a forgotten god. When you first equip this wand, roll Comprehend, DC34. On a failure, the wand disappears, reappearing back in a space belonging to you in 24 hours. On a success, the wand slips tendrils of chaotic, twisting magic into your mind, and you parse through ten thousand strands of fantastical magic to find the wand’s true identity, successfully activating it. While this wand is active, you can cast Nondetection as a Basic spell with a +4. If you’re so scared of being perceived that you need a legendary wand to make it go away altogether forever, though, you should try therapy.

NAME: VASHNAR’S SCEPTER OF THORNS
DESCRIPTION: A tall, thick staff made of haunting soulleaf wood bound together with a vine from the abyssal bloom, a massive tree with only one known to exist in the world, said in old stories of folk magic to have roots that grow straight down into the demonic plane. When you first equip this wand, it attacks you, lunging and snaring you with tangling vines. Roll Agility and Endurance, both DC21, and if you fail either, you must Resist, DC18. If you succeed on both, you overpower the wand, and you successfully activate it. While this wand is active, you can take a +8 to Herbalism, increase your cultivation capacity by 1000, and you can access your cultivated magic plants from anywhere, as the wand’s abyssal bloom vine comes to life and responds to your commands, spectrally connecting to the plant you want from anywhere on any plane. If you unequip this wand and are over your cultivation capacity as a result, you have 24 hours to get rid of the plants or reequip this wand before the excess plants die.

NAME: BLACKWEB VOID
DESCRIPTION: An icy-cold staff made of nularchanite, an unnaturally blacker-than-black metal with powerful antimagic properties found only in SP-UL3342102, a plane where no magic is known to function. When you first equip this wand, roll Recall Information, DC27. On a failure, an icy wave of antimagic flows from the wand and melts through your mind, blocking out magic, and you forget every spell you know for 24 hours and cannot attempt to equip this wand again until then. On a success, you resist the force of antimagic, and you successfully equip the wand. While this wand is active, any spell cast on you that you do not choose to be affected by, if it is cast with a roll less than 20, has no effect. You can use the wand to cast Dispel at will with a roll of 30, and you can activate the wand to create a 1-meter-radius antimagic field as in the spell Nullity Field. You can either place the antimagic field on a fixed point for 24 hours or keep it surrounding you for 30 minutes, but either way, once you do, you cannot use this ability again for 24 hours. Casting antimagic on your homework doesn’t make it go away, though.

NAME: GLACIOCORPUS
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of crimson ice enchanted with the blood of a water wyrm. While this wand is active, you can take a +6 to all Evocation spells that would cause someone to Resist, and you can activate the wand’s freezing hexological power to chill the blood inside a target’s body. Roll Evocation + 6, DC23, and on success, the target must roll Endurance versus your check or Resist Fear and Wound, both DC16. You must Resist when you use this ability, DC16. If this ability reduces somebody to 0 Resolve, they are frozen from the inside out, and they will not regain Resolve unless you choose to release the effect and allow them to.

NAME: SCRIBACORDA
DESCRIPTION: A small, elegant wand with a classically simple design, made of broken-heart oak woven with nightmare mane hair. While this wand is active, you can take a +6 to any Read Person check to determine a person’s relationships and how they feel about other people, including yourself. You can activate the wand’s power against a target and roll Deception or Persuasion, + 6, DC30. On success, the target must roll Recall Information against your check, and on failure, you rewrite their feelings and memories about people. For each degree of failure on their check, you can implant an idea of a different type of relationship with one person in their head, which they will believe with false memories to back it up, e.g. making someone believe they’re friends with someone they’ve never met, making someone believe they hate a close friend, or making someone no longer know a person they know and trust. You must Resist when you use this ability, DC18.

NAME: PUPPETMASTER
DESCRIPTION: A deep royal violet wand forged from the bone of a creature killed by an evil-eyes, bound up in passionoak weave until the hex-steeped bone is hidden away. When this wand is active, you can enchant magic onto people as you can on objects without their consent. This takes ten minutes longer than enchanting a spell onto an object. You can also activate the wand against a target and roll Enchantment, DC26. On a success, the target must roll Willpower against your spell check or be possessed for five minutes per degree of failure on their check. While you have a target possessed, you can psychically control them as an extension of yourself. Once the possession wears off, they will have no memories of the time they were possessed. You must Resist when you use this ability, DC20.

NAME: MIND THIEF
DESCRIPTION: A heavy, ornate wand made of a deep pink alloy of metals from a far-flung plane of hive-mind consciousness, etched with runic markings that fill up with radiant blue magic when it’s stolen someone’s abilities. While this wand is equipped, you can use it on a target and roll Sleight of Hand, DC25. On success, the target must roll Recall Information versus your check or have one Ability of your choice stolen. Their Ability score suffers a -2 penalty for each degree of failure on their check, and as long as you have this wand equipped, you can take a bonus on that Ability equal to their penalty. You must Resist when you use this ability, DC21. If you use this ability while the wand already has a bonus active, the new one will overwrite the previous one, and the person suffering from the penalty will have their Ability score restored. You could also just try improving your own abilities instead of stealing somebody else’s, but I guess that wouldn’t be as fun.

NAME: AVIS METUS
DESCRIPTION: A thick staff made of metusburn, a reviled tree full of hexological fear magic that’s nearly been wiped from existence but still exists in isolated clusters mostly on other planes, and crowned with a heavy iron cap in the shape of a small bird taking flight. While this wand is active, you have a +4 on checks to Resist Fear, and if you force someone to Resist Fear, they have a -4 penalty to the roll. You can activate the wand’s magic to summon a small spectral bird that streaks towards a target and implants itself in their mind. Roll Enchantment, DC27. On a success, the target must roll Agility or Willpower versus your check or be inflicted with a new debilitating fear. Choose one thing per degree of failure on their check for them to become afraid of, and they will need to roll to Resist Fear any time they encounter that thing, with a DC at the GM’s discretion (5-25), higher the more rare and specific the fear. You must Resist Fear when you use this ability, DC25. You cannot use this ability if you have any stacks of Fear.

NAME: HAYWORTH’S PEN OF LIES
DESCRIPTION: A dark, sleek black-onyx wand enchanted with a drop of unicorn’s blood, with a tip like a pen nib that appears loaded with the unicorn’s blood. While this wand is active, you can cast the spell Mindweaver with a +6 bonus, and the spell only costs 1000 materials. The wand is pretty stylish, all things considered, so honestly, gaslighting has never looked so good.

NAME: SPINNER OF DARK FATES
DESCRIPTION: A jet-black wand made of dark lunar platinum, a version of the precious tychomantic lunar platinum corrupted by hexological magic, connected to the fabric of destiny. While this wand is active, at the start of every day, the GM will present you with two situations that might occur over the day, and you can choose which one will take place (although it is not a guarantee that the other will not). You can also activate the wand’s power and roll Arcane History, DC35 to weave a curse into somebody’s fate. On success, you can choose one person per degree of success (including potentially the same one multiple times), and at some point over the next 24 hours, each person chosen will end up in a situation where they have to Resist, DC17. When you use this ability, you will also end up in a situation where you have to Resist, DC17, over the next 24 hours, whether you succeeded on your check or not. You cannot use this ability again until the 24 hours are up.

NAME: IRRESISTIBLE SPARK
DESCRIPTION: A long, thin wand in pure ivory white, steeped for three days and three nights in the dreamscape of a person locked in a hexological nightmare. While this wand is active, you can take a +8 to any Charm skill, an ability which must be declared before rolling. If the roll still fails, the target knows you tried to manipulate their mind, and if it fails with two or more degrees of failure, they know it was with hexological magic. Whenever you use this ability, you must Resist, DC10.

NAME: CURSED FOOTSTEPS
DESCRIPTION: A long staff like a walking stick, made of griffin yew fired in Ignus flame, with a hooked tip. The claw-foot bottom end leaves a dark shimmer on the ground wherever it touches that disappears shortly after, but lingers invisibly and undetectably waiting to trap someone. While this wand is active, any time someone in a space you’ve been in tries to detect traces of you in that space with divination or chronomancy instead triggers the hexological magic left behind, flaring up and ensnaring them in a dark web. They must Resist, DC20, and they fail to detect you.

NAME: SONG OF BROKEN FATE
DESCRIPTION: A wand made of blood-red broken-heart oakwood, touched by the terrible presence of a true demon. When you first equip this wand, you must make a DC30 Willpower roll or be possessed by a true demon's spirit for 1 hour and fail to equip the wand. While this wand is active, you can take a +6 bonus to Arcane History, and at the start of each day, you can see three things that are on course to happen over the day, and you can choose to make any of them guaranteed or impossible to happen. In addition, when you see a check about to take place, you can draw on the power of fate and reduce this wand's condition by 1000 materials in order to decide the check succeeds or fails, regardless of the DC.
